title: Fix: Prevent error in lldb `kernel vmmap`
body:
Summary of the fix:

- In the LLDB backend, vmmap used to proceed to compute a kernel/user memory map path when there were no known user mappings, which could trigger the kernel vmmap path and lead to errors due to invalid LLDB values during KernelVmmap.adjust.
- The change ensures that if there are no known pages (get_known_pages yields an empty set), vmmap returns an empty LLDBMemoryMap right away, avoiding the problematic kernel-vmmap path entirely.
- A dedicated test was added (tests/lldb-tests/tests/test_command_vmmap.py) to verify that vmmap executes without error in LLDB and gracefully handles cases with no memory mappings.

Impact:
- Prevents crashes when no mappings are known but kernel-vmmap would otherwise be invoked.
- Keeps behavior predictable for LLDB users when there are no mappings to display.

Fixes #3102

step_plan.summary: Fix LLDB vmmap to avoid kernel vmmap path when there are no known memory mappings by returning an empty LLDBMemoryMap early.

steps.operation: Add guard in LLDBProcess.vmmap to short-circuit and return an empty LLDBMemoryMap when there are no known pages
steps.reason: To avoid triggering the kernel/vmmap path and KernelVmmap.adjust when there are no known user mappings, preventing errors due to invalid LLDB values
steps.side_effects: No mappings scenario will now be stable; downstream consumers get an empty map. Tests that assume non-empty mappings may need updates or additional coverage for the empty-map case. Minor API surface remains unchanged for non-empty mappings.
